zh.lifeSummary: 當Oda d’Avesnes, Dame d’Avesnes, Leuze et Condé於1040年之後出生在法國豪-德-福朗斯諾爾德艾維斯-素-爾時，她的父親Baudouin V comte de Flandre et de Hainaut8987歲，母親Adèle de France8990歲。她於1060年與Baudouin de Gand, seigneur d’Alost結婚。他們至少是3個女兒的父母。她去世於1082年4月24日在法國諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德奧易希。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Oda d’Avesnes, Dame d’Avesnes, Leuze et Condé于1040年之后出生在法国豪-德-福朗斯諾爾德艾維斯-素-爾时，她的父亲Baudouin V comte de Flandre et de Hainaut8987岁，母亲Adèle de France8990岁。她于1060年与Baudouin de Gand, seigneur d’Alost结婚。他们至少是3个女儿的父母。她去世于1082年4月24日在法国諾爾-帕-德-卡拉斯諾爾德奧易希。

nameMeanings.name: Dame
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and French: from Middle English, Old French dame ‘lady’ (from Latin domina ‘mistress’), applied as a nickname for a foppish young man whose outfit and manner have an effeminate elegance, or as a metonymic occupational name for someone in the service of a lady. As a French name it may also be a nickname meaning ‘mother-in-law’.
English: possibly an East Anglian variant of   Dam   4.

variants.name: Dam
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Hoa, Tuan, Ngoc, Thanh, Binh, Chanh, Duc, Hiep, Minh, Nu, Tien.  Southeast Asian Hong, Chan, Dong, Hon, Man, Oi, Ok, Phoung, Sang, Sokhom, Sun.
Vietnamese (Đàm): from the Chinese surnames 譚, 谈 and 覃, see Tan 1-3.
Cambodian: written ដាំ, possibly of Vietnamese origin (see above). The surname ដាំ also corresponds to the Khmer word meaning e.g. ‘to plant’.

nameMeanings.name: Leuze
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Othmar.
German: variant of   Lenz  .

variants.name: Lenz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Erwin, Kurt, Gerhard, Hans, Frieda, Hannelore, Heinz, Helmut, Horst, Aloys, Baerbel.

German: from a personal name, in which two originally distinct names have fallen together: from a pet form of the personal name Lorenz , and the ancient Germanic personal name Lanzo, which was originally a short form of any of several compound names with land ‘land’ as the first element, e.g. Lambrecht (see Lambert ). This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Poland, and Czechia. Compare Lentz .

German: in some cases, a nickname from Middle High German lenze ‘spring’ from lang ‘long’, since in this season the days grow longer. The name may also have been bestowed on someone who was born in the spring or who owed rent or service at that time of year, or it may have denoted someone who was of a sunny and spring-like disposition.

nameMeanings.name: et
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Anders, Erik, Knut.
Swedish: topographic or ornamental name from ek ‘oak’.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of several farms named Eik, from eik ‘oak’.

variants.name: Eck
meanings: German, Dutch, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): topographic name from Middle High German ecke, egge ‘corner’, German Eck(e) (Yiddish ek). This could have been the corner of two streets or, in the case of the German name, the corner of a field or area of land or a rocky outcrop.

German: from a short form of any of various ancient Germanic compound personal names with the first element agi(n), agil ‘point, edge (of a sword)’, akin to the word in 1 above. This surname (in both senses; see 1 above) is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine).
Swedish and Norwegian: variant of   Ek  .

nameMeanings.name: Condé
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Manuel, Luis, Juan, Pedro, Raul, Jorge, Ruben, Angel, Cesar, Fernando.  Portuguese Goncalo, Manoel.
Spanish and Portuguese: nickname from the title of rank conde ‘count’, a derivative of Latin comes, comitis ‘companion’. See Conte .
English (Shropshire): variant of Cundy , a cognate of 3 below, or Cound, a habitational name from Cound in Shropshire.

variants.name: Conte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Mario, Angelo, Salvatore, Antonio, Rocco, Carmine, Pasquale, Luigi, Silvio, Silverio, Giuseppe, Sal, Alfonso, Biagio, Carlo, Emilio, Fernando, Gerardo.

Italian: from the title of rank conte ‘count’, from Latin comes, genitive comitis ‘companion’. Probably in this sense (and in the Late Latin sense of ‘traveling companion’), it was a medieval personal name. As a title it was no doubt applied ironically as a nickname for someone with airs and graces or simply as a metonymic occupational name for someone in the service of a count. Compare Lo Conte .
Spanish: variant of   Conde   ‘count’ (see also 1 above).

variants.name: Candee
meanings: Americanized form of French Condé (see Conde ) or, alternatively, Candé, a habitational name from Candé in Maine-et-Loire, or from Candé-sur-Beauvron in Loire-et-Cher, or southern French Condy .
Alternatively, perhaps an altered form of English Condy , a cognate of the name Candé in 1 above.

History: The Candees trace their origin to Zaccheus Cande(e), who in the middle of the 17th century resided in the New Haven County, CT, and his probable father John Condy alias Conde. The name of the latter is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors as Jean/John Candée/Cande/Condé and in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America as John Conde. See also   Conde  .

nameMeanings.name: Oda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Apparently a short form of any of the given names ending in these letters, for example,   Fiona   and   Anona  , now well established as an independent given name.

en.lifeSummary: When Oda d’Avesnes, Dame d’Avesnes, Leuze et Condé was born after 1040, in Avesnes-sur-Helpe, Nord, Hauts-de-France, France, her father, Baudouin V comte de Flandre et de Hainaut, was 8987 and her mother, Adèle de France, was 8990. She married Baudouin de Gand, seigneur d’Alost in 1060. They were the parents of at least 3 daughters. She died on 24 April 1082, in Oisy, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Oda d’Avesnes, Dame d’Avesnes, Leuze et Condé est née après 1040, à Avesnes-sur-Helpe, Nord, Hauts-de-France, France, son père, Baudouin V comte de Flandre et de Hainaut, avait 8987 ans et sa mère, Adèle de France, avait 8990 ans. Elle a épousé Baudouin de Gand, seigneur d’Alost en 1060. Ils ont eu au moins 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 24 avril 1082, à Oisy, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Oda d’Avesnes, Dame d’Avesnes, Leuze et Condé nach 1040 in Avesnes-sur-Helpe, Nord, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Baudouin V comte de Flandre et de Hainaut 8987 und ihre Mutter Adèle de France 8990. Sie hat 1060 Baudouin de Gand, seigneur d’Alost geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 24. April 1082 in Oisy, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Oda d’Avesnes, Dame d’Avesnes, Leuze et Condé è nata il dopo il 1040 nel Avesnes-sur-Helpe, Nord, Hauts-de-France, Francia, sua padre, Baudouin V comte de Flandre et de Hainaut, aveva 8987 anni e sua madre, Adèle de France, aveva 8990 anni. Lei sposato Baudouin de Gand, seigneur d’Alost nel 1060. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figlie. Morì il 24 aprile 1082, nel Oisy, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Oda d’Avesnes, Dame d’Avesnes, Leuze et Condéが1040年以後にフランスハウトス-デ-フランスノードエイベスネス-スル-ヘルペで生まれた時，父Baudouin V comte de Flandre et de Hainautは8987歳，母Adèle de Franceは8990歳であった。1060年にBaudouin de Gand, seigneur d’Alostと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1082年4月24日にフランスノード-プアス-デ-カレイスノードオイシで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Oda d’Avesnes, Dame d’Avesnes, Leuze et Condé(이)가 1040년 이후 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 노르드 에베스네스-수-헬프에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Baudouin V comte de Flandre et de Hainaut(은)는 8987세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Adèle de France(은)는 8990세였다. 그녀는 1060년 Baudouin de Gand, seigneur d’Alost(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1082년 4월 24일 프랑스 노르드-파스-데-캘레이 노르드 이시에서 사망했다.

pt.Aalst: Aalst, Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Oda d’Avesnes, Dame d’Avesnes, Leuze et Condé nasceu após 1040, em Avesnes-sur-Helpe, Nord, Hauts-de-France, França, seu pai, Baudouin V comte de Flandre et de Hainaut, tinha 8987 anos e sua mãe, Adèle de France, tinha 8990 anos. Ela casou-se com Baudouin de Gand, seigneur d’Alost em 1060. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 24 de abril de 1082, em Oisy, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Oda d’Avesnes, Dame d’Avesnes, Leuze et Condé родилась после 1040 г. в местности авеснес-сур-хелп, норд, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция, её отцу по имени Baudouin V comte de Flandre et de Hainaut было 8987 лет, а матери, которую звали Adèle de France, 8990. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Baudouin de Gand, seigneur d’Alost в 1060 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 дочери. Она умерла 24 апреля 1082 г. в местности оиси, норд, норд-пас-де-калеи, Франция.

es.Aalst: Aalst, Brabante del Norte, Países Bajos
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Oda d’Avesnes, Dame d’Avesnes, Leuze et Condé nació después de 1040, en Avesnes-sur-Helpe, Nord, Hauts-de-France, Francia, su papá, Baudouin V comte de Flandre et de Hainaut, tenía 8987 años y su mamá, Adèle de France, tenía 8990 años. Se casó con Baudouin de Gand, seigneur d’Alost en 1060. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijas. Murió el 24 de abril de 1082, en Oisy, Nord, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia.
